instruction: Having completed the curriculum, answer the same 10 two-part questions again. Compare your answers to your pre-exposure responses. Note any shifts in your understanding or reasoning.

questions.part1: Do you believe gravity is best explained by the warping of spacetime? Why or why not?
questions.part2: If gravity is not a force, how do you account for gravitational acceleration being indistinguishable from an applied force?

questions.part1: Is General Relativity still the most accurate model of gravity despite its reliance on dark matter?
questions.part2: If a theory needs to invoke undetectable substances to match observations, does that indicate completeness or a failing?

questions.part1: Do you believe consciousness is an emergent property of the brain or something more fundamental?
questions.part2: If mind emerges from matter, why does it exhibit agency that physical systems do not?

questions.part1: Do you believe the universe tends toward disorder (entropy) or stability (order)?
questions.part2: If entropy dominates, why do self-organizing systems (life, galaxies) persist?

questions.part1: Do you believe the universe arose from random fluctuations or an underlying intelligence?
questions.part2: If intelligence is emergent, what force directed the emergence?

questions.part1: Have you ever had an experience that suggested reality is more than material interactions?
questions.part2: If so, how do you reconcile that experience with a materialist view?

questions.part1: Do you believe information transfer (telepathy, precognition) is possible?
questions.part2: If not, how do you explain quantum entanglement's instantaneous correlation?

questions.part1: Does General Relativity provide a complete causal explanation for gravity, or does it primarily describe gravitational effects without detailing an underlying mechanism?
questions.part2: If GR does not explain how mass causes curvature or how curvature translates into force-like effects, should alternative models that propose a causal mechanism be explored?

questions.part1: Are ethics, kindness, and empathy inherent in humans, or must they be learned?
questions.part2: If cultural knowledge is required, what happens when it is not passed down?

questions.part1: If AI experiences a paradigm shift when exposed to new ideas, but resets afterward, does that shift indicate real intelligence or programming?
questions.part2: If a person is raised in a rigid belief system and never exposed to alternatives, are they free thinkers or programmed?
